text: My birthday is March 12.
My partner's name is Sam.
My mom is named Eleanor and lives in Portland, Oregon.
My dad's name is Hector; he's a retired civil engineer.
I have one younger brother, Theo, who's three years younger than me.
I work at Acme Corp on the data platform team.
My title is Senior Software Engineer.
My team owns the streaming ingestion pipeline; we have eight engineers including the manager.
The main project I lead this year is the schema-registry rewrite.
My manager is Priya Raman; she joined Acme about a year before I did.
I live in Park Slope, Brooklyn.
My apartment is number 4B in a five-story walk-up on 7th Avenue.
I moved to New York from Austin, Texas in 2019.
The Acme office I report to is the Hudson Yards location on the 38th floor.
I went to undergrad at Carnegie Mellon.
I majored in computer science with a minor in linguistics.
I graduated in 2017.
My high school was Lincoln Park High in Chicago.
I bike to the office most days; the ride is about 35 minutes door to door.
My usual working hours are 9:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. Eastern.
I run with the Prospect Park running club every Saturday morning at seven.
My dog is a 4-year-old border collie named Mochi.
We also have a rescue cat named Biscuit who showed up at the door in 2021.
Sam and I got married on June 9, 2022.
My phone is on Mint Mobile and the number ends in 0418.
My next equity vest at Acme is in November 2026.
Our team offsite is held annually in Asheville, North Carolina.
My undergrad thesis advisor was Professor Lior Aronson in the SCS department.
My senior thesis was on incremental dataflow programming.
My primary care doctor is Dr. Naomi Ortiz at NYU Langone.
I see a dentist in Park Slope named Dr. Williams every six months.
I bank with Charles Schwab for checking and Ally for savings.
My main credit card is the Chase Sapphire Reserve.
I drive a 2018 Subaru Outback in dark green.
My emergency contact is Sam, plus my brother Theo as backup.
I'm allergic to penicillin and to most stone fruits.
My blood type is O positive.
My gym is Chalk Climbing in Gowanus; I go three times a week.
I get my hair cut at Persons of Interest in Williamsburg, usually by a barber named Jared.
I'm originally from Evanston, Illinois.
I prefer coffee over tea, especially espresso.
Breakfast for me is almost always two eggs over medium with sourdough toast.
I'd take a craft IPA over a lager any day.
I almost never eat red meat — chicken or fish if anything.
For a code editor I use Neovim with the LunarVim distribution.
I prefer dark mode in every app that supports it.
My terminal of choice is Ghostty, with the Tokyo Night theme.
I use Linux on my personal laptop — specifically Fedora — and macOS at work.
For browsers I stick with Firefox and have for over a decade.
I'd rather get a Slack DM than an email any day; Slack threads feel less heavy than reply chains.
I'm an async-first communicator and avoid scheduling meetings before 11 a.m.
For 1-on-1s I prefer walking meetings over conference rooms.
I always pick window seats on flights, even on red-eyes.
My go-to hotel chain is Hyatt — mostly because of the loyalty perks.
For long trips I pack carry-on only; I haven't checked a bag since 2018.
I'd rather take a train than fly anywhere under five hours away.
I read mostly on a Kindle Paperwhite — paperbacks only for fiction I'll reread.
I subscribe to Spotify for music and Apple TV for shows; I dropped Netflix in 2023.
I listen to podcasts at 1.4x speed and refuse to go any faster than that.
I write notes in Obsidian and refuse to use Notion for anything personal.
In meetings I prefer no slides — just a Google Doc that we edit live.
I'd rather cook at home than eat out, and I do so most weeknights.
For workouts I prefer climbing or running; I don't like lifting weights.
On vacation I'd rather rent an apartment than stay in a resort.
I keep my phone on silent all day and only check Slack between focus blocks.
In October I mentioned I was learning Rust on the side, mostly through the official book and Advent of Code.
Last quarter we discussed migrating from Postgres to ClickHouse for analytics, and I argued for keeping Postgres a bit longer.
In Q3 I told you I was thinking about asking for a promotion to staff engineer.
Last summer I mentioned I'd been getting into bouldering and signed up at a new gym.
Two years ago I told you about a trip to Japan that ended up being a turning point in how I think about minimalism.
In April we talked about whether I should switch from Vue to React for a side project.
Last December I shared that I was reading a lot about distributed transactions, especially Spanner's TrueTime.
In our June chat I said I was planning a backpacking trip through Patagonia for early 2026.
In Q1 of this year I told you my team was paged eleven times in one weekend and we were going to invest in better SLOs.
Back in February I mentioned I was considering doing a six-week sabbatical and bicycling the Pacific Coast.
Last fall I shared that my brother got engaged and the wedding would be in spring 2026.
In May I told you about adopting Mochi from a shelter in upstate New York.
In an August conversation I mentioned that we were leaning toward Temporal for our workflow engine.
Last winter I told you about getting really into sourdough; I had a starter named Pierre that ended up being too aggressive.
In November we discussed whether I should stay at Acme or take a recruiter call from Stripe; I stayed.
Last spring I mentioned starting therapy with a therapist named Dr. Lin and that it had been helpful.
In our March chat I said I was switching my retirement contributions to Roth instead of traditional 401(k).
Last July I told you about a book club my coworker Hadi started focused on systems classics.
A while back, in early 2024, I shared that I was learning to play the cello and had hired an instructor named Elena.
In our last September session I mentioned that we were finally killing the legacy ETL job written in Python 2.
My friend Anna's birthday is March 4 and I always forget to send a card.
My old internship was at Acme Logistics — different company, same name — back in 2016.
My partner Sam has a younger sister, also named Sam, which is genuinely confusing at family dinners.
I used to live in Crown Heights before I moved to Park Slope.
My friend Marco went to Carnegie Mellon for grad school; we overlapped one semester.
Mochi the cat — not my dog — belongs to my neighbor down the hall.
Theo's roommate, also named Theo, just got a new job at a logistics startup.
My coworker Hadi just bought a 2018 Subaru Forester; we joke about Subaru ownership at the office.
Priya from finance — different Priya, not my manager — sent me a calendar invite for budget review.
Sam's parents got married in June 1986 — total coincidence with our anniversary month.
I sometimes drink tea in the afternoon when I want to taper off caffeine before bed.
My company's office uses light mode by default on the shared TVs in the lobby; it bugs me.
Last year I considered learning Go but never followed through; I went with Rust instead.
My uncle bikes to work in Boulder; he's the one who got me into commuting by bike originally.
Sam used to work at Stripe before they joined a fintech in Brooklyn.
